docs.title: 🐍 Python Project Template
docs.text:
Welcome to the Python Project Template repository! This template is designed to help you quickly set up a Python project with essential tools and best practices for code quality, security, complexity analysis, testing, and deployment. Whether you’re starting a new project or looking to standardize your development workflow, this template provides a solid foundation.

For more details about the tools and configurations used in this project, see the Content page.

docs.title: 📰 Features
docs.text:
• Code Quality Tools: Integrated with Black for automatic code formatting, Flake8 for linting, and Mypy for static type checking.
  • Security Analysis: Uses Bandit to identify common security issues in your Python code.
  • Code Complexity Analysis: Integrated with Complexipy to measure and analyze the complexity of the codebase.
  • Testing: Built-in support for Pytest for running tests and ensuring code correctness.
  • Documentation: Uses MkDocs for generating beautiful documentation websites, with the Material for MkDocs theme.
  • Deployment: Easily deploys documentation to GitHub Pages.

docs.title: 🏗️ Installation and Setup
docs.text: Setting up your development environment with this project template is quick and easy. Just follow these steps:

docs.title: Clone the Repository
docs.text:
First, clone the repository to your local machine:

git clone https://github.com/yourusername/python-project-template.git
cd python-project-template

docs.title: Activate Your Python Environment
docs.text: Next, activate your Python virtual environment. This will depend on your operating system and the environment created.

docs.title: Install Dependencies and Set Up the Project
docs.text:
This template includes a Makefile that automates the setup process, including installing dependencies and preparing the environment. To run the setup, simply execute:

make

This will automatically install all the required dependencies and set up the environment for linting, testing, security analysis, and documentation.

docs.title: 💛 Contributing
docs.text: Feel free to fork this template and adapt it to your needs! Contributions are always welcome. If you’d like to contribute, please submit a pull request or open an issue.

docs.title: Project Files and Their Functions
docs.text: This section explains the main files and their roles in the Python Project Template.

docs.title: Makefile
docs.text:
The Makefile automates common tasks:

  • install: Installs or upgrades Poetry and project dependencies.
  • clean: Removes __pycache__ and .pytest_cache to clean up.
  • lint: Runs code quality checks with Black, Mypy, and Flake8.
  • test: Runs tests using Pytest.
  • wiki-up: Serves documentation locally with MkDocs.
  • pre-commit: Ensures code is clean and formatted before committing.
  • all: Runs clean, lint, test, and wiki-up to prepare the project.

docs.title: pyproject.toml
docs.text:
This file configures Poetry and other tools like Black, Flake8, Mypy, and MkDocs:

  • Project Info: Defines the project name, version, and description.
  • Dependencies: Lists test, linting, and documentation dependencies like pytest, black, flake8, and mkdocs.
  • Black Configuration: Sets line length to 89 characters and prevents string normalization (quote changes).
  • Flake8: Configures line length, warnings to ignore, and which errors to check for.
  • Mypy: Configures type checking options, including ignoring missing imports and treating optional types strictly.
  • Pylint: Sets line length and disables specific warnings (e.g., duplicated code).
